user_message: Quick one -- draft the follow-up on Azure Ridge Partners. This is time-sensitive -- customer is waiting.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Thomas Okafor <thomas.okafor@azureridgepa.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-05 10:28 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Azure Ridge Partners executive brief

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about executive brief. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: Thomas Okafor <thomas.okafor@azureridgepa.com>
Date: 2024-02-18 09:17 EST
Subject: executive brief - Action Required

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about executive brief. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Thomas Okafor <thomas.okafor@azureridgepa.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-21 09:20 EST
Subject: executive brief - Action Required

Just checking in on the executive brief proposal. Our CFO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

Meeting: executive brief Discussion - Azure Ridge Partners
Date: 2024-01-25
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Thomas Okafor (Azure Ridge Partners), Raj Mehta (Microsoft), Priya (Azure Ridge Partners)

[00:02:00] Thomas Okafor (Azure Ridge Partners): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on executive brief from our last session.

[00:08:00] Priya (Azure Ridge Partners): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about technical validation. Can we address those?

[00:10:00] Thomas Okafor (Azure Ridge Partners): The timeline for resource allocation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:14:00] Priya (Azure Ridge Partners): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about integration complexity. Can we address those?

[00:17:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about cost optimization. Can we address those?

[00:21:00] Priya (Azure Ridge Partners): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is cost optimization.

[00:23:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is integration complexity.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Pre-Meeting Brief template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct executive brief decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
